cases.resplendent: brilliantly attractive and impressive, splendid in appearance
cases.axiomatic: self-evidently true, not needing to be proved
cases.atrocity: an extremely cruel and violent act
cases.squeamish: easily disgusted or made queasy by unpleasant things
cases.discernment: the ability to judge well, keen perception
cases.eschatological: relating to death, judgment, and the final destiny of the soul
cases.dilettante: a person with an amateur or superficial interest in an art or subject
cases.stoic: enduring hardship without complaint, showing no emotion
cases.unflappable: having calm self-control even in a crisis
cases.fulcrum: the central point on which something turns or depends
cases.ramification: a consequence of an action, especially one that is complex
cases.extricate: to free oneself from a difficult situation
cases.purgatory: a state of suffering or torment, especially temporary
cases.bulwark: a defensive wall, or a person or thing that provides strong support
cases.harbinger: a person or thing that signals the approach of something
cases.subjugate: to bring someone under domination or control by force
cases.subsume: to include something within a larger category or group
cases.maraud: to roam in search of things to steal or people to attack
cases.sitting duck: a person or thing with no protection against attack
cases.sacrilege: violation or disrespect of something considered sacred
cases.proponent: a person who advocates for a theory or course of action
cases.covetous: having a strong desire to possess something belonging to another
cases.concubine: a woman who lives with a man of higher status without being his wife
cases.kindred spirit: a person whose interests or attitudes closely resemble one's own
cases.prolific: producing a great quantity or number of something
cases.bankroll: to provide the funds needed for an enterprise
cases.fizzle out: to end weakly or fail after a promising start
cases.gone belly up: to fail completely, especially of a business
cases.tenacious: holding firmly to something, persistent and determined
cases.epigone: a less distinguished follower or imitator of an important predecessor
cases.piquant: having a pleasantly sharp taste or appealingly stimulating quality
cases.can of worms: a complicated problem that would be better left untouched
cases.bemoan: to express regret or dissatisfaction about something
cases.bespoke: made specifically to a customer's order, custom-made
cases.affluent: having a great deal of wealth
cases.arid: extremely dry, or lacking interest and passion
cases.foreswear: to renounce or give up something under oath
cases.rime: a white frost formed by freezing fog
cases.hoarfrost: a white deposit of feathery ice crystals formed on cold surfaces
cases.regale: to entertain someone with an engaging story or account
cases.caprice: a sudden and unaccountable change of mood or behavior
cases.orderly: an attendant in a hospital responsible for nonmedical duties
cases.straitjacket: a restrictive garment used to confine the arms of a violent person
cases.prole: a member of the working class, used dismissively
cases.ramshackle: poorly made or maintained, rickety and shabby
cases.accost: to approach and confront someone boldly
cases.acumen: the ability to make good judgments and quick decisions in a field
cases.ward: a section of a hospital devoted to a particular kind of patient care
cases.garrotte: a wire or cord used to strangle someone
cases.brawl: a rough or noisy fight or quarrel
cases.delusion of grandeur: an exaggerated belief in one's own importance or power
